nativeRange.habitat: Shaded, soft, acidic blackwater streams of peninsular Malaysia, growing rooted in leaf-litter and detritus-rich sediments along slow-moving watercourses.

avoidWith: Silver Dollars and other plant-eating fish

summary: A Malaysian rosette Cryptocoryne prized for its quilted, often deep purple to reddish leaves. Generally considered intermediate-difficulty and best suited to the midground of soft, acidic planted tanks.
careNotes: Cryptocoryne nurii is a rosette Cryptocoryne native to peninsular Malaysia, where it grows along shaded, soft, acidic blackwater streams in leaf-litter-rich sediments. It is highly regarded in the planted-tank hobby for its distinctive quilted (ruffled) leaf texture and deep purple-to-reddish coloration, which is often most intense on the leaf undersides. Aquarium Co-Op describes the plant as reaching up to 20 cm tall, with leaf color ranging from bronze to green depending on lighting and fertilization. Several recognized trade variants exist, including 'Mutated', 'Pahang Mutated', and 'Rosen Maiden', which differ in the intensity of leaf coloration and the degree of leaf surface texturing. Lighting is generally advised to be low to moderate; higher light tends to bring out the reddest coloration but can also drive algae. CO2 injection is not strictly required but is commonly reported to improve growth, leaf size, and color saturation. Cryptocoryne nurii is a heavy root feeder, so a nutrient-rich substrate and/or regular root tabs near the root zone are strongly recommended. Plant by burying the roots up to (but not over) the crown, which can rot if buried. CRYPT MELT WARNING: this species is generally considered more melt-prone than the very tolerant C. wendtii / C. beckettii group and tends to demand soft, acidic, and stable conditions to settle in. After planting or any major water-parameter change, it commonly sheds most or all of its existing leaves; the rhizome typically pushes new, adapted leaves over 2-6 weeks, so the plant should be left in place during a melt event. Placement is mid-ground in standard-height tanks, or background in nano tanks. SOURCE NOTE: this entry was completed from a single primary source (Aquarium Co-Op product page) — the configured secondary (Buce Plant) returned 404 at access time and no further fallback was attempted under the agent's single-fetch safety budget. Water-parameter ranges (temperature 22-28 °C, pH 5.5-7.0, soft gH/kH) reflect typical Malaysian-blackwater Cryptocoryne husbandry consensus rather than per-source measurement, and the careLevel of 'intermediate' reflects this species' reputation as more demanding than C. wendtii / C. beckettii. Set to needs_review so a human reviewer can confirm or tighten the water parameters and difficulty against a second source.
breedingNotes: Propagated primarily by runners (stolons) that produce daughter plantlets along a horizontal rhizome; once a daughter has developed several leaves and a healthy root system, it can be cut from the runner and replanted. Rhizome division is also possible — cut a healthy rhizome into sections each retaining leaves and roots. Like other Cryptocoryne, C. nurii can flower (producing the spathe-and-spadix inflorescence typical of Araceae) when grown emersed, but seed propagation is not practical in the home aquarium.

primary.name: Aquarium Co-Op
primary.notes: Aquarium Co-Op product page for Cryptocoryne nurii tissue culture. Confirmed up to ~20 cm height, low-light tolerance, nutrient-rich substrate / root-tab feeding, midground (tall tanks) or background (nano tanks) placement, bronze-to-green color variation under light/fertilizer, and easy-to-care-for reputation under stable conditions.
